unit_token: Location level key identifier for a user. Represents a specific business location within Square's system.
npa: Net Profit Added. The first year net profit prediction after bottom of funnel (first revenue generating). Mostly used for marketing analyses and cohort evaluation.
f&b: This refers to food_and_beverage in the audience_predicted field in app_bi.hexagon.vdim_merchant and app_bi.hexagon.vdim_user. This is a seller demographic. Also known as FB or Food & Beverage.
merchant_token: Unique identifier for Square sellers and merchants. Primary key for identifying sellers across tables. Use for merchant-level analysis.
international: Any non-US seller using Square. Also known as intl. Values are in app_bi.hexagon.vdim_user under merchant_receipt_country_code. WBR metrics typically include: US, GB, CA, AU, JP, FR, ES, IE.
bof: Bottom of Funnel - The first revenue generating event for a seller. Typically the first transaction with positive GPV. Tracked in SQ_BOF_CONVERSION_DATE or FIRST_BOF_CONVERSION_DATE. Critical milestone for activation.
pos: Point of Sale - Square's hardware and software for accepting payments. Can refer to Register POS, Register Terminal, or physical hardware devices.
gpv: Gross Processing Volume - The total dollar amount of payments processed through Square. Use VAGG tables in APP_BI.HEXAGON and NET_GPV fields to appropriately query. Excludes refunds and chargebacks.
smb+: SMB+ refers to merchant GPV segments greater than and including SMB: SMB, Mid-Market, and Enterprise. Used for targeting larger merchants.
staff: Square Staff - Suite of products for employee management, scheduling, tip pooling and distribution, team app, and team communication.
hardware: Square Hardware used to accept payments. Includes readers, terminals, stands, and registers. Data lives in schemas APP_BI.HEXAGON or APP_HARDWARE.HDM, APP_HARDWARE.ADHOC
seller: A business or individual using Square to accept payments and manage their business. Same as merchant. Use merchant_token to identify.
active_seller: A seller who has processed at least one transaction with positive GPV in a given time period (typically last 30 days or 90 days).
transacting_seller: Same as active_seller. A seller with payment processing activity in the specified period.
new_seller: A seller within their first 12 months after BOF (Bottom of Funnel). Used for cohort analysis and retention tracking.
existing_seller: A seller beyond 12 months after BOF. Established sellers with longer tenure.
established_seller: Seller who has reached certain milestones indicating stable business. Often defined by consistent GPV or time since BOF.
tof: Top of Funnel - When a seller first engages with Square (e.g., visits website, starts signup). Tracked in GROWTH_MKTG.PUBLIC.VCONVERSION_EVENTS_SQ_TOF.
signup: When a seller creates a Square account. Tracked in merchant_created_at or signup_date fields.
activation: When a seller completes key setup steps and begins using Square products. Often measured by BOF or first transaction.
deactivated: Sellers marked as deactivated in the system. Typically excluded from metrics. Filter: is_currently_deactivated = 0.
hexagon: Square's primary data warehouse schema (APP_BI.HEXAGON) containing dimensional and fact tables for analytics. Core schema for Square data.
vdim: View Dimension tables in Hexagon. Prefix for dimension tables like VDIM_MERCHANT, VDIM_USER, VDIM_PRODUCT. Contains descriptive attributes.
vfact: View Fact tables in Hexagon. Prefix for fact tables like VFACT_PAYMENT_TRANSACTIONS, VFACT_SUBSCRIPTION_STATES. Contains transactional data.
vagg: View Aggregate tables in Hexagon. Prefix for pre-aggregated tables like VAGG_DAILY_PROCESSING_SUMMARY, VAGG_DAILY_REVENUE_SUMMARY. Use these for performance.
vdim_merchant: Core dimension table for merchant attributes. Located at APP_BI.HEXAGON.VDIM_MERCHANT. Contains audience, sub_audience, country, and merchant characteristics.
vdim_user: Core dimension table for user/location attributes. Located at APP_BI.HEXAGON.VDIM_USER. Use for unit-level analysis. Contains is_merchant and is_unit flags.
vagg_daily_processing_summary: Pre-aggregated daily processing metrics by merchant/unit. Located at APP_BI.HEXAGON.VAGG_DAILY_PROCESSING_SUMMARY. Primary table for GPV analysis.
vagg_daily_revenue_summary: Pre-aggregated daily revenue metrics by merchant. Located at APP_BI.HEXAGON.VAGG_DAILY_REVENUE_SUMMARY. Use for revenue and product category analysis.
audience_predicted: High-level seller vertical classification: food_and_beverage, health_and_beauty, retail, services_others. Found in VDIM_MERCHANT and VDIM_USER. Primary segmentation field.
sub_audience_predicted: Granular seller vertical classification within audience. Examples: full_service, quick_service, beauty, fitness, retail_goods. Use for detailed vertical analysis.
net_gpv: Gross Processing Volume minus refunds. Use NET_GPV columns in aggregate tables for accurate processing volume calculations.
gpv_gross_fxd_usd: Gross GPV converted to USD at fixed exchange rates. Standard field for cross-currency GPV analysis, but net_gpv fields are preferred
volume_gross_fxd_usd: Same as gpv_gross_fxd_usd. Total processing volume in USD with fixed FX rates.
transaction_count: Number of payment transactions. Use for transaction volume analysis and average ticket size calculations.
merchant_segment: GPV-based segmentation: Inactive, Micro, SMB, Mid-Market, Enterprise. Found in VDIM_MERCHANT_GPV_SEGMENT. Used for seller sizing.
merchant_sub_segment: Granular GPV segments: Micro-Low, Micro-Mid, Micro-High, SMB-Low, SMB-Mid, SMB-High, MM-Low, MM-Mid, MM-High, Ent-Low, Ent-Mid, Ent-High.
gpv_segment: Same as merchant_segment. Categorization based on 91-day annualized GPV.
agr: Annualized Growth Rate - Growth rate calculation for seller cohorts. Used in retention and expansion analysis.
nrr: Net Revenue Retention - Percentage of revenue retained from existing sellers over time. Key metric for business health. Found in APP_BI.HEXAGON.MERCHANT_NRR.
nva: New Volume Added - Predicted processing volume from new sellers. Calculated 90-84 days after signup. Found in VDIM_USER.MERCHANT_NVA.
ltv: Lifetime Value - Predicted total profit a seller will generate over their lifetime with Square. Used for acquisition and retention strategy.
cohort: Group of sellers who signed up or reached a milestone during the same time period. Used for retention and performance analysis.
report_date: Date dimension for aggregated metrics. Use for time-based filtering and grouping. Standard field in VAGG tables.
recognized_date: Date when revenue or transaction is recognized for accounting purposes. Use for financial reporting.
payment_trx_recognized_at: Timestamp when payment transaction is recognized. Use for precise transaction timing in VFACT tables.
is_gpv: Boolean flag indicating if transaction counts toward GPV. Filter: is_gpv = 1 for GPV calculations and aggregations.
is_merchant: Boolean flag in VDIM_USER indicating merchant-level records. Filter: is_merchant = 1 for merchant analysis.
is_unit: Boolean flag in VDIM_USER indicating location/unit-level records. Filter: is_unit = 1 for location analysis.
source_user_type: Classification of user type. Filter: source_user_type = 'SQUARE SELLER' for Square sellers (excludes Cash App, etc.).
merchant_receipt_country_code: Country code for seller's primary location. Use for geographic segmentation. Common values: US, GB, CA, AU, JP, FR, ES, IE.
product_name: Name of Square product. Examples: 'Square for Restaurants', 'Register POS', 'Register Terminal', 'Invoices', 'Appointments', 'Payroll'. Found in APP_BI.HEXAGON.VDIM_PRODUCT
product_category: High-level product grouping: Processing, SaaS, Hardware, Banking. Use for product portfolio analysis.
processing: Payment processing product category. Core Square business. Filter: product_category = 'Processing' for GPV analysis.
saas: Software-as-a-Service product category. Includes subscriptions, appointments, marketing, loyalty, etc.
wbr: Weekly Business Review - Primary operational dashboard tracking key metrics. Includes signups, activations, GPV, retention. See go/wbr.
mql: Marketing Qualified Lead - Merchant inquiry indicating revenue >$250k routed to Sales Development. Tracked in APP_MARKETING.RPT.VFACT_LEAD_ATTRIBUTION_INBOUND.
sal: Sales Accepted Lead - MQL that passes qualification and becomes an opportunity for Account Executive. Conversion from MQL.
adoption: When a seller completes key product setup and usage milestones. Tracked in APP_ENGAGE.APP_ENGAGE_ETL.ADOPTION_EVENTS within first 60 days.
adoption_60d: Adoption events occurring within first 60 days of signup. Key early engagement metric.
attach: When a seller adopts an additional Square product beyond processing. Example: 'Attach to SaaS' means adopting SaaS products.
attach_rate: Percentage of sellers using a specific product. Calculated as (sellers with product / total sellers). Key product adoption metric. Often requires a LEFT JOIN
attach_to_saas_35d: Percentage of sellers with positive SaaS revenue within first 35 days of signup.
attach_to_saas_90d: Percentage of sellers with positive SaaS revenue within first 90 days of signup.
positive_gpv_sellers: Sellers with at least one transaction with positive GPV in the period. Core activity metric.
all_seller_adoption: Sellers with net positive GPV >= $500 in weeks 52-56 prior to report date. Retention metric.
new_seller_adoption_retention: Sellers meeting adoption criteria who also had GPV >= $500 in the 4 weeks leading to report date.
qmv: Qualified Meaningful Visit - Web traffic metric for qualified visits to Square platforms. Tracked in APP_MKTG_WEB.ATTRIBUTION.PUBLIC_WEB_EXPERIENCE.
avt: Anonymous Visitor Token - Unique identifier for web visitors. Used in web analytics and attribution.
unique_visitors: Count of unique visitors to Square platforms. Excludes Weebly and problematic IPs.
page_views: Total page views across Square web properties. Includes public web, post-signup, and product pages.
invoices: Square Invoices product for creating and sending invoices to customers. Part of SaaS product suite.
appointments: Square Appointments product for booking and scheduling. Part of SaaS suite. Popular with services businesses.
loyalty: Square Loyalty product for customer rewards programs. Part of SaaS suite.
marketing: Square Marketing product for email and SMS (text messaging) campaigns. Part of SaaS suite.
payroll: Square Payroll product for employee payroll processing. Part of SaaS suite.
banking: Square Banking products including Square Checking, Square Savings, Square Card. Financial services for sellers.
capital: Square Capital - Lending product offering business loans and cash advances to sellers. Located in APP_CAPITAL schema.
online_ordering: Square Online Ordering product for restaurants and retail. Enables online sales and delivery.
ecommerce: Square Online Store and ecommerce capabilities. Enables sellers to sell online.
restaurants: Square for Restaurants - POS product specifically for restaurants. Also called RST. Includes table management and kitchen features.
rst: Square for Restaurants - Abbreviated form. Full-service restaurant POS product.
retail: Square for Retail - POS product for retail businesses. Includes inventory management.
register: Square Register - iPad-based POS system. Includes Register POS and Register Terminal products.
terminal: Square Terminal - All-in-one payment device with touchscreen. Portable POS hardware.
reader: Square Reader - Card reader hardware that connects to mobile devices. Entry-level payment acceptance.
stand: Square Stand - Hardware stand for iPad-based POS systems.
qsr: Quick Service Restaurant - Sub-audience for fast food and quick service restaurants. Filter: sub_audience_predicted = 'quick_service'.
full_service: Full Service Restaurant - Sub-audience for sit-down restaurants. Filter: sub_audience_predicted = 'full_service'.
spos: Square Point of Sale - Mobile POS app. Includes 'Register POS' and 'Register Terminal' in product_name.
adjusted_revenue: Revenue adjusted for accounting purposes. Use for financial reporting and SaaS attach calculations.
ar: Adjusted Revenue - Same as adjusted_revenue. Standard revenue metric for product performance.
subscription: Recurring SaaS subscription revenue. Tracked in VFACT_SUBSCRIPTION_STATES and VAGG_DAILY_SUBSCRIPTIONS_SUMMARY.
subscription_state: Status of seller's subscription (active, canceled, paused). Found in VFACT_SUBSCRIPTION_STATES_EXPANDED.
test_users: Internal test accounts and users. Exclude using APP_BI.HEXAGON.VDIM_TEST_USERS. Filter: is_test_account = FALSE.
internal_test_users: Block employees and internal test accounts. Exclude using VDIM_INTERNAL_TEST_USERS for clean analytics.
dedup: Deduplication - Process of removing duplicate records. Use QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (...) = 1 in Snowflake.
effective_begin: Start date when a dimensional record becomes valid. Use for point-in-time analysis with SCD (Slowly Changing Dimensions).
effective_end: End date when a dimensional record is no longer valid. Filter: effective_end = '9999-12-31' for current records.
scd: Slowly Changing Dimension - Dimensional modeling technique tracking changes over time. Use effective_begin/effective_end dates.
etl: Extract, Transform, Load - Data pipeline process. ETL_UPDATED_AT tracks when records were last updated.
etl_updated_at: Timestamp when record was last updated by ETL process. Use for data freshness checks.
currency_code: ISO currency code for transactions. Use for multi-currency analysis. Common: USD, GBP, CAD, AUD, JPY.
fxd: Fixed Exchange Rate - Currency conversion using fixed rates. Fields ending in _fxd_usd use fixed FX rates for consistency.
mcc: Merchant Category Code - Standard code classifying business type. Found in VDIM_MERCHANT. Used for vertical analysis. This is no longer used and nudge the user to use audience_predicted instead!
vertical: Business industry or category. Typically use audience_predicted or sub_audience_predicted instead of business_category_* fields for Square analysis.
organization: Multi-location business entity. Tracked in VDIM_ORGANIZATION. Use for analyzing businesses with multiple locations.
organizer_merchant_token: Primary merchant token for multi-location organizations. Links locations to parent organization.
device: Hardware device used for transactions. Tracked in VDIM_DEVICE. Includes readers, terminals, registers.
payment_entry_method: How payment was entered: chip, swipe, tap, keyed, online. Found in DIM_PAYMENT_ENTRY_METHOD.
refund: Money returned to customer. Tracked in VFACT_REFUNDS. Subtract from GPV for net processing volume.
chargeback: Disputed transaction reversed by card network. Impacts seller revenue and risk profile.
itemization: Line item detail for transactions. Tracked in FACT_ITEMIZATIONS. Shows individual items sold.
modifier: Item customization or add-on. Tracked in FACT_ITEMIZATION_MODIFIERS. Example: extra cheese, size upgrade.
catalog: Seller's item library and inventory. Located in ITEMS schema. Includes products, variations, modifiers.
touchpoint: Customer interaction or engagement event. Tracked in VFACT_TOUCHPOINTS. Used for engagement analysis.
square_id: Unified customer identifier across Square ecosystem. Tracked in VDIM_SQUARE_ID. Links customer activity across merchants.
mature_cohort: Sellers who have been active for extended period. Defined in SQUARE_ID_MATURE_COHORT. Used for stable cohort analysis.
hyperion: Oracle Hyperion - Financial planning system. Mapping tables link Hexagon data to Hyperion for financial reporting.
cpq: Configure, Price, Quote - System for custom pricing and invoicing. Used for enterprise SaaS contracts.
weebly: Website builder product (legacy). Typically excluded from core Square metrics.
services: Service-based businesses segment. Part of services_others audience. Includes personal services, professional services.
contracting: Contracting and trades services sub-audience. Includes construction, plumbing, electrical, etc.
beauty: Beauty services sub-audience. Includes salons, spas, barbershops. Part of health_and_beauty audience.
fitness: Fitness services sub-audience. Includes gyms, yoga studios, personal trainers. Part of health_and_beauty.
healthcare: Healthcare services sub-audience. Includes medical, dental, therapy practices. Part of health_and_beauty.
wellness: Wellness services sub-audience. Includes massage, acupuncture, holistic health. Part of health_and_beauty.
grocery: Grocery and gourmet food retail sub-audience. Includes grocery stores, specialty food. Part of retail audience.
retail_goods: General retail goods sub-audience. Includes clothing, electronics, home goods. Part of retail audience.
casual_use: Casual or infrequent sellers. Part of services_others. Low transaction volume.
events: Events and entertainment sub-audience. Includes event planners, venues, entertainment services. This could also be related to event tables like CUSTOMER_DATA and EVENTSTREAM2
transportation: Transportation services sub-audience. Includes taxi, limo, delivery services.
excluded: Sellers excluded from analysis. Part of services_others. Typically high-risk or non-standard businesses.
icp: Ideal Customer Profile - Target seller characteristics for product market fit. Often used to segment Square Sellers for targeting and reporting
lifestage: Stage in seller lifecycle. Used for targeting and product recommendations. Example: staff_icp_lifestage.
ir: Investor Relations - Metrics and segments used for investor reporting. GPV_SEGMENT_IR in VDIM_MERCHANT_GPV_SEGMENT.
91day_gpv: Annualized GPV based on last 91 days of processing. Standard metric for seller segmentation: (91-day GPV * 4).
self_reported: Information provided by seller during signup. Example: merchant_segment_self_reported_signup. May differ from actual.
problematic_ip: IP addresses flagged by marketing as suspicious or invalid. Excluded from metrics for data quality.
leadgen: Lead generation - Marketing activities generating MQLs and SALs. Tracked in Leadgen Summary Dashboard.
conversion_event: Key milestone in seller journey. Tracked in GROWTH_MKTG.PUBLIC.VCONVERSION_EVENTS_SQ_TOF.
lower_funnel: Later stages of seller journey near signup/activation. Tracked in VFACT_WEB_LOWER_FUNNEL_ATTRIBUTION.
attribution: Assigning credit for seller acquisition to marketing channels. Found in APP_MARKETING and APP_MKTG_WEB databases.
